steps.title: Identify the defining cross-cultural finding
steps.content: The study's key result is “similar correlations between patterns of these factors and of personality traits” across all 53 countries. This means the relationship between personality traits and musical preferences remained consistent across cultures.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A expresses what the finding directly implies: people who share personality traits tend to prefer similar Western music across cultural contexts. The other options either import unsupported elements (B extends to non-Western music, D invokes familiarity) or make claims without evidence (C compares to prior assumptions the passage never mentions). That matches choice A — A is correct.
